[[Solomon Haumono]] (uncle) }} }} '''David Fifita''' ({{langx|to|Tēvita Fifita}}) (born 28 June 1989) is a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[The Entrance Tigers]] in the [[Central Coast Division Rugby League|Central Coast League]] in NSW, Australia. He previously played for the [[Lezignan Sangliers]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He has spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[2017 RFL Championship|Championship]]. ==Background== He was born in [[Blacktown|Blacktown, New South Wales]], Australia. He attended [[Patrician Brothers' College, Blacktown]] and [[Blacktown Boys High School]]. Fifita is of [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]] and [[Demographics of Tonga|Tongan]] descent and played his junior rugby league for [[Glenwood, New South Wales#Rugby League|St Patricks Blacktown]] and [[Doonside Roos]], before being signed by the [[Wests Tigers]]. Fifita is the twin brother of fellow [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] player [[Andrew Fifita]], and cousin of [[Gold Coast Titans]] player [[David Fifita (rugby league, born 2000)|David Fifita]]{{cite web|author=Chris Barrett|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/brotherly-shove-fifita-double-act-ready-to-give-opponents-trouble-20101021-16w19.html|title=Brotherly shove: Fifita double act ready to give opponents trouble|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=21 October 2010|accessdate=2015-10-18}} He is not related to [[John Fifita]], a former St George Dragons player.{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/greenberg-keen-to-sign-graham-to-longterm-deal-20130713-2pwnl.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Greenberg keen to sign Graham to long-term deal}} Fifita's elder brother [[Latu Fifita]] plays for [[Workington Town]] in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Kingstone Press Championship]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/latu-fifita-goes-from-wild-child-to-brink-of-nrl-with-cowboys/story-fni3g8pa-1226847739593|title=Latu Fifita goes from wild child to brink of NRL with Cowboys|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=2014-03-06|accessdate=2016-09-05}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== In 2009, Fifita played for the Wests Tigers' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team{{cite web |url=http://rleague.com/players/David-Fifita_2485/Matches/ |access-date=4 February 2016 |title=David Fifita |publisher=rleague.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140317105442/http://rleague.com/players/David-Fifita_2485/Matches/ |archive-date=17 March 2014 }} In 2010, he played for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] alongside his brother. In 2011, he moved on to the Tigers' [[New South Wales Cup]] teams, [[Balmain Tigers#Club today|Balmain Ryde-Eastwood Tigers]] and [[Western Suburbs Magpies]]. At the end of 2012, he joined the [[Lézignan Sangliers]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].{{cite web|author=|url=http://stevemascord.com/2013/06/19/the-battle-for-david-fifita/|title=The Battle For David Fifita –|publisher=Stevemascord.com|date=2013-06-19|accessdate=2015-10-18}}{{Dead link|date=September 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} In June 2013, after finishing his season with Lézignan, he attracted the interest of NRL clubs, [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and [[Melbourne Storm]]. [[File:davidfifita.jpg|thumb|right|Fifita playing for the Sharks in 2013]] He eventually signed a contract with the Sharks for the rest of the [[2013 NRL season]], the club where his twin brother [[Andrew Fifita|Andrew]] played.{{cite web|author=Michael Carayannis|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/fifita-has-ground-to-make-up-to-catch-twin-brother-20130709-2poa4.html|title=Fifita has ground to make up to catch twin brother|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=9 July 2013|accessdate=2015-10-18}} After playing 6 games for the Sharks in the NSW Cup, he re-signed with them for 2014.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/david-fifita-twin-of-cronulla-suoerstar-prop-andrew-scores-nrl-contract-with-sharks/story-fni3gol8-1226708218493|title=David Fifita Twin of Cronulla Superstar Prop Andrew Scores NRL Contract with Sharks|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2015-10-18}} Fifita was a member of the 2013 NSW Cup premiership side which defeated Windsor Wolves 38-6 in the grand final.{{Cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/fair-go-for-david-fifita-in-nrl-at-cronulla-sharks-20140322-hvlla.html|title=Fair go for David Fifita in NRL at Cronulla Sharks|date=22 March 2014}} ===2014=== In Round 2 of the [[2014 NRL season]], Fifita made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for Cronulla-Sutherland against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.sharks.com.au/news/2014/03/17/late_mail_sharks_v_bulldogs.html|title=Late Mail - Sharks v Bulldogs|publisher=Sharks|date=17 March 2014|accessdate=2015-10-18}} On 17 July 2014, he re-signed with Cronulla-Sutherland on a two-year contract.{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.sharks.com.au/news/2014/07/17/sharks_secure_talented_pair.html|title=secure talented pair|publisher=Sharks|date=16 July 2014|accessdate=2015-10-18}} ===2015=== On 13 February 2015, Fifita played for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] against the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] in the [[2015 All Stars match]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/final-all-stars-teams-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/83748/default.aspx|title=Final All Stars teams announced|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-02-09|accessdate=2015-10-18}} In October, Fifita was selected in Tonga's 48-man train-on squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/tonga-named-preliminary-train-on-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/90108/default.aspx|title=Tonga name preliminary train-on squad|date=25 September 2015|publisher=NRL.com|accessdate=21 October 2015}} He would go on to make the final 18-man squad where he would be named the 18th man and would fill in for any late injuries, suspensions or tactical changes.{{cite web|url=http://www.asiapacificrl.com/2015/10/09/tonga-finalise-squad-for-world-cup-qualifier/|title=Tonga finalise squad for World Cup Qualifier|date=9 October 2015|publisher=asiapacificrl.com|accessdate=19 October 2015}} He would go on to play in that match, in what was only his second appearance for Tonga, after filling in for [[Leilani Latu]]. ===2016=== On 7 May, Fifita played for Tonga against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the 2016 [[Polynesian Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/samoa-holds-on-in-pulsating-pacific-test/tabid/10874/newsid/95681/default.aspx|title=Samoa holds on in pulsating Pacific Test|date=7 May 2016|publisher=nrl.com|accessdate=7 May 2016}} On 10 June, after gaining a release from [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]], he joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-year contract, with an option for a second year, effective immediately. Fifita turned into a big fans favourite at [[Wakefield Trinity]] due to his physical dominance in the middle of the park.{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/sharks-release-fifita-22320/#yicEZQMrhtTu3J1Z.97|title=Sharks release Fifita|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=10 June 2016|accessdate=2016-06-10}}{{cite web|last=Patterson|first=Mike|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/10309390/wakefield-sign-prop-david-fifita-from-cronulla-sharks|title=Wakefield sign prop David Fifita from Cronulla Sharks | Rugby League News|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2016-06-10|accessdate=2016-06-14}} ===2017=== Fifita made 21 appearances for Wakefield Trinity in the [[2017 Super League season]] where the club shocked many by finishing fifth on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/12/super-league-awards-complete-review-2017-season/|title=2017 Super League review|website=www.theguardian.com|date=12 October 2017}} ===2018=== In the [[2018 Super League season]], Fifita made 17 appearances for Wakefield as the club finished 7th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/england/article/53794/season-review-wakefield-trinity/|title=Wakefield Trinity 2018|website=www.rugby-league.com}} ===2019=== Fifita played 22 games for Wakefield in the [[2019 Super League season]] where the club finished ninth on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/survival-specialists-wakefield-trinity-rlfc-2019-season-review-23277/|title=Wakefield Trinity RLFC 2019 Season Review|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=15 October 2019}} ===2020=== On 28 August, Fifita was stood down by Wakefield after he refused to wear a GPS tracking device for coronavirus protocols.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12058427/wakefields-david-fifita-stood-down-for-refusing-to-wear-gps-tracker/|title=Wakefield's David Fifita stood down for refusing to wear GPS tracker|website=www.skysports.com}} Fifita played only nine games for Wakefield in the [[2020 Super League season]] as the club finished second bottom.{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-review-5-positives-2020-3080714/|title=Wakefield Trinity review - 5 positives from 2020|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}} ===2021=== Fifita played 25 games for Wakefield Trinity in the 2021 Super League season which saw the club finish in 10th place.{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/2407/season-review-wakefield-trinity/|title=SEASON REVIEW: WAKEFIELD TRINITY|website=www.superleague.co.uk}} ===2022=== In round 18 of the 2022 Super League season, Fifita scored a try and was later sent to the sin bin during Wakefield's 38-26 loss against [[Toulouse Olympique]] at [[Magic Weekend]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12196/12648675/super-league-magic-weekend-day-1-at-newcastles-st-james-park-live/|title=Saints, Toulouse and Leeds all win on Magic Weekend|website=www.skysports.com}} After seven seasons at Wakefield Trinity, Fifita announced that he would be departing the club at the end of the year. Fifita's final game for Wakefield was a 16-14 loss against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/wakefield-trinity-star-david-fifitas-massive-offers-from-super-league-clubs-58579/|title=Wakefield Trinity star David Fifita's 'massive offers' from Super League clubs|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}} After leaving Wakefield he joined [[The Entrance Tigers]] in the [[Central Coast Division Rugby League]]. ===2023=== In May, it was announced that Fifita would come out of semi-retirement to play for Wakefield Trinity after the club had lost its opening 13 matches. Fifita made his return in the clubs [[Magic Weekend]] loss against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in round 14. In round 15, Fifita played in Wakefield's 24-14 upset victory over Leeds to end a 14-game losing streak.{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leave-a-bigger-legacy-behind-david-fifita-determined-to-cement-name-in-wakefield-trinity-folklore-4179641/|title='Leave a bigger legacy behind': David Fifita determined to cement name in Wakefield Trinity folklore|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}} Fifita played a total of 12 matches for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}} ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==External links== {{commons category|David Fifita (rugby league, born 1989)}} *[https://wakefieldtrinity.com/player/david-fifita/ Wakefield Trinity profile]
